title: [phase:critique] [family:ui-runtime-review] [provider_hint:subagent] Review the current Happy production resolution. Facts: production /happy/ serves updated HTML with Live Session wired to /cockpit; /cockpit returns 200; unauthenticated GET /api/happy/state,/events,/task-detail return 401 by design...
summary_text: 1. **High (Blocker)**: Refresh flow can still misclassify backend/auth-state failures and strand sessions after token consumption. `rotateRefreshToken` consumes (deletes) the old refresh token before caller-visible error mapping, and collapses non-token errors to `null`; `handleRefreshToken` then returns `401 Invalid refresh token`. Also, after rotation succeeds it performs a second auth-state lookup and can return `404` after already minting a new refresh token, leaving client without the new token. [jwt-auth.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts):323 [jwt-auth.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts):328 [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts):522 [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts):530 2. **Medium**: Login/refresh rate limiting remains race-prone under concurrency (non-atomic KV read-then-write), which weakens brute-force protection exactly on newly protected auth endpoints. [rate-limiter.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/rate-limiter.ts):143 [rate-limiter.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/rate-limiter.ts):169 [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts):415 [cockpit-api.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts):512 3. **Medium (Operational Risk)**: New smoke login script defaults to remote production target and remote D1 writes by default, increasing accidental production mutation risk when run without explicit env overrides.
